migration_notes: LOCAL MODEL ARCHITECTURE (the spine every crosswalk row follows): Neutral core.* services carry party-anchored REFERENCES + metadata only (no PII). PHI/PII lives in healthstack.* overlay schemas via cross-schema FK to core.* with LEFT JOIN read views. This is enforced by the 6-layer PHI boundary (@curaos/healthstack-phi-boundary): PG role isolation, FHIR-only access, APISIX route guard, Opengrep CI import-ban, Presidio egress scrub, reference-only events. Migration MUST preserve this boundary.

TENANCY + OWNERSHIP KEYS: composite ownership keys - (tenant_id) neutral, (tenant_id,user_id) personal, (tenant_id,org_id) business. Citus shard key = tenant_id. AsyncLocalStorage TenantContext + Postgres search_path session. Every entity row is tenant-scoped; personal entities add user ownership (person owns), business add org.

GENERATOR-FIRST MIGRATION PATH (binding per curaos-generator-evolution-rule): landing order is feature -> codegen template/emitter (THE MOLD at curaos/tools/codegen) -> @curaos/contracts (TypeSpec .tsp + AsyncAPI) -> @curaos/<name>-sdk (generated client) -> service controller/Drizzle schema -> FE via @curaos/api-client. NEW domain models go in the SERVICE's specs/<name>.tsp, NOT the mold (the mold ships only health/protected/protected-write/whoami probes). Edits that should apply to EVERY future service belong in tools/codegen/templates/service-core/specs/.

MATURITY REALITY (from inventory + spec scan): MOST services are contract-defined-but-thin (scaffold mold = carrier with probe endpoints only, the {id} read route left FREE for the domain to add). REAL domain models exist in: identity (auth full), workflow-core + business-workflow (BPM), automation-core (low-code+connectors+runs), sales-core (quote/order/invoice/commission/revenue), hr-core (employee/comp-ABAC/leave/time), crm-core (account/contact/deal), inventory-core (event-sourced stock ledger + UDI/lot/recall), procurement-core (PO/receipt/invoice/3-way-match/budget), donation-core (campaign/pledge/recurring/receipt), esign-core (signing/signature/verify wet|pkcs7|xades|qualified), event-core (public event/registration/capacity/recurrence + domain event bus), geospatial-core (point/geofence/route/nearest), fleet-core (vehicle/asset/dispatch/maintenance), documents-core (versioning/retention/checksum/presigned), tenancy-core (tenant/isolation-mode), healthstack-billing (invoice/estimate/payment), healthstack-consent (grant/revoke/signature), healthstack-messaging, personal-tracking (position/geofence/SOS), personal-notes (note/folder/attachment), builder-core (surface/block/theme DSL), plugin-runtime (plugin/extension-point/config-hook), terminology (coding suggestion/FHIR JSON).

CLINICAL GAPS requiring generator-first build (highest priority for FHIR-R4 alignment): patient PHI overlay (healthstack.patients - DOB/SSN/gender/name/telecom), Practitioner+PractitionerRole, Encounter (encounter-service has sdk+mold but no domain model), Observation/Vital (no model; terminology can code LOINC), Condition/Problem (healthstack-problems scaffold), Procedure (only CPT code flows in billing seam), ServiceRequest order (orders-service sdk+mold), DiagnosticReport lab (healthstack-lab scaffold), ImagingStudy (healthstack-imaging scaffold; RAPTOR protocol/contrast-safety is differentiating completeness), MedicationRequest/Medication (healthstack-meds scaffold), AllergyIntolerance (no model; feeds meds+imaging safety), CarePlan+Goal+CareTeam (healthstack-careplans scaffold; THE person-journey spine), Coverage/InsurancePlan (only payerRef/payerPlanId IDs today). All should align to FHIR-R4 + US Core profiles, dual-code ICD-10-CM+SNOMED / LOINC / CPT-HCPCS / RxNorm-NDC via terminology-service.

MONEY DISCIPLINE: all monetary values are integer minor units (cents) stored as int64/bigint (sales/procurement/billing/donation already do this; non-TS SDKs warned about int32 truncation on high-quantity lines). Any new financial entity MUST follow this. Currency is 3-char ISO.

TRUST MODEL (P0-3, consistent across ALL writes): actorId + tenantId are ALWAYS JWT-derived, NEVER body-supplied. The mold rejects body-supplied actorId. Write bodies are strict Zod (e.g. only optional reason 1..512). whoami is the header-trust proof endpoint. New entity write DTOs must follow this (no actor/tenant in body).

SOFT-DELETE + AUDIT INTEGRITY: deactivated_at/deleted_at semantics (no hard delete) so overlay rows + audit hash-chain stay referentially valid. documents-core blocks delete during retention (409 DeleteConflict). Audit is tamper-evident SHA-256 hash-chain (audit-core-service, IHE BALP-aligned, per-resource audit_chain_heads, dual-write until full migration). Every service emits curaos.core.audit.event.v1 (reference-only, no PHI).

RBAC + ABAC: Role enum = tenant-admin|user|clinician|support-agent|auditor|break-glass-admin (identity-service). ABAC for sensitive fields (HR Compensation.baseSalary redacted to null for non-HR-managers is the model pattern). Break-glass logged with reason; privilege escalation requires approval. PHI reads gated by 6-layer boundary. SMART-on-FHIR scopes recommended for clinical API authz on migration.

PARTY-ANCHORING (DRY identity, eliminates the source CRMs' duplicate-contact problem): one core.parties row is reused across ALL person/org roles - Patient (1:1 partyId), Practitioner, Employee (no PII in HR), CRM Account/Contact, Customer, Supplier, Donor, esign Signer, Beneficiary. Odoo res.partner / Dolibarr societe prove the unified-party completeness; CuraOS already enforces it. Build party-core REAL FIRST (it is the upstream anchor everything FKs).

REUSE-OVER-PARALLEL (per curaos-reuse-dry-rule): Referral = ServiceRequest + Task (not a new entity); Carrier = Organization + InsurancePlan; clinical Order = ServiceRequest, ERP PurchaseOrder = distinct entity (do not conflate); Activity = Task + calendar event (polymorphic parent, not parallel call/meeting/email types); CarePlan.activity + Referral both reference the ONE tasks-core Task aggregate; medical-supply dispense reuses inventory issueStock; drug-as-product reuses commerce product + inventory SKU; one neutral documents-core plane serves clinical (DocumentReference)+CRM+ERP attachments; GDPR consent reused tenant-wide.

ASYNCAPI GAP (generator-first follow-up): codegen emits .tsp -> OpenAPI 3.1 via @hey-api but does NOT auto-gen .asyncapi.yaml; event schemas are hand-curated. Add AsyncAPI emission to the generator so the event contract (curaos.<layer>.<domain>.<entity>.<event>.vN, reference-only, outbox-relayed, seq-dedup) is generated, not hand-written. Also DOMAIN_ROUTE_MAP is hand-curated (not auto-discovered from contracts) - a known generator gap.

BIGGEST SCAFFOLD-TO-REAL LIFTS (effort-ranked): (1) accounting-core double-entry GL/CoA/Tax (Odoo account.move model is the blueprint; auto-post from already-emitted sales/procurement/payment events - simplify business by removing manual journal entry); (2) the clinical FHIR-R4 resource set (Encounter/Observation/Condition/Procedure/ServiceRequest/DiagnosticReport/ImagingStudy/MedicationRequest/AllergyIntolerance/CarePlan/Coverage) - large but generator-first + terminology-backed; (3) commerce-core Product/Service (Medusa v2 MikroORM tier per ORM rule) which PriceList + SalesOrder fulfillment depend on.

reference_systems.Execution/State/Retry/Error: Activepieces FlowRun/StepRun(output) + n8n ExecutionEntity(status,mode) + Windmill job + Node-RED Message/Context (CuraOS run-step status/duration/error real; add explicit retry-backoff + DLQ)

workflow_automation_alignment.fhir_clinical_workflow: Clinical workflows define-able as FHIR PlanDefinition/ActivityDefinition + executed via Task; BPMN-aligned. Event notification -> FHIR Subscription(R4)/SubscriptionTopic(R5).
workflow_automation_alignment.person_centric_role: Per LENS, workflows are built around the customer/patient JOURNEY (personal-workflow = person's own orchestration), org/compliance process satisfied as a consequence (business-workflow attaches management/approval steps to the person-journey spine). This is the strongest embodiment of the person-centric directive.

person_centric_lens_summary.binding_directive: Every entity yields a DUAL SURFACE on the SAME data + contract: a person-facing surface (personal-*/patient-app: simple, journey-driven, the person owns their data + journey) AND a management surface (business-*/healthstack-*/clinician/admin: full compliance/reporting capability). No feature loss - simplification means re-centering + automation + defaults, never dropping a business/management/compliance capability.
person_centric_lens_summary.ownership_pattern: personal-* = person is subject AND owner; *-core = neutral journey+data+contract spine both surfaces build on; business-*/healthstack-* = management surface whose workflows still organize around the person served. Party-anchoring + composite (tenant,user) personal keys + consent-as-person-owned + patient-owns-PHI (charter) encode this in the data model.
person_centric_lens_summary.mining_rule: External corpora (Odoo/ERPNext/Dolibarr/OpenEMR/OpenMRS/OpenHospital/VistA/SuiteCRM/EspoCRM) are mined for COMPLETENESS (data models, business rules, compliance, validation, revenue-cycle, terminology, edge cases) - NOT for their org-first/admin/form-heavy UX, which is re-centered on the person.
